board.designation: DB-FA1 — Fire alarm panel sub-DB, IEC commercial 3-storey office
board.location: adjacent to building main entrance — FA installer/serviceman access point

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSB-MAIN F04 (63A MCB Type C, 60m feeder, NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 §560)

selectivity_results.code_clause: IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 — 63A Type C MCB upstream selective against ≤10A Type C MCBs at DB-FA1 (6.3:1 current ratio at FA05 worst case; ≥10.5:1 at FA01/FA02/FA03/FA04/FA06). Same-curve cascade supports time-current selectivity per IEC 60898-1 cascade table. Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table at 9 kA fault level.

non_compliance_flags.message: DB-FA1 carries life-safety load — IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 recommends a secondary supply path via ATS. Current design relies on the integral panel battery for primary backup; ATS to a generator or alternative supply should be considered if the panel battery capacity does not meet the AHJ-required survival duration (typically 24h standby + 30 min alarm).

Neutral current 4.6 A is 62.2% of the largest phase (L2 = 7.4 A). Per IEC 60364-1 § 132.6: single-phase load distribution best practice — rebalance recommended. Re-allocate one or more circuits between phases to reduce neutral loading and improve voltage symmetry.

assumptions: DB-FA1 is the fire alarm panel sub-DB — `role: fire_alarm_panel` flagged in the board block.
IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 (life-safety/standby supply systems) governs the entire FA distribution: NO upstream RCD, dedicated circuit isolation, fire-rated cables, secondary supply integration.
ALL circuits use `voltage_class: "fire_alarm"` to signal segregation requirements to downstream consumers (riser, cable-containment, segregation-distance constraints).
FP200 LSZH fire-rated cable specified for all FA circuits — provides 30/60/120-minute fire survival depending on cable class (per IEC 60331 / BS EN 50200).
IEC 62820 fire detection & alarm system standard governs the panel design + zone loop topology; this DB schedule covers only the upstream electrical supply.

NO RCD anywhere in the DB-FA1 chain (including upstream at MSB-MAIN F04) per IEC 60364-5-56 §560.7.2 — earth-fault trip on a life-safety circuit risks losing fire-detection capability during a fire involving cable insulation degradation.
Estimated PFC at DB-FA1 ≈ 9 kA after 60m 16mm² SWA feeder impedance from 25 kA at MSB-MAIN; 10 kA Icn MCBs cover this without cascade backup.
All Type C MCBs (Ia = 10×In) — handles capacitive inrush of FA panel SMPS + sounder bank energisation.

drawing_notes: DB-FA1 located adjacent to the building main entrance — chosen for fire-brigade and FA installer access.
Fed from MSB-MAIN F04 via 60m 16mm² 5-core SWA XLPE Cu feeder (FP200 alternative possible if the feeder route passes through unprotected escape routes — engineer to confirm fire-strategy zoning).
6 circuits + 2 spare ways (W7-W8 reserved — typical future expansion includes additional sounder-strobe circuit for atrium acoustic coverage, or interface circuit to access-control / lift-recall).
Role: fire_alarm_panel — flagged in board block for downstream SLD/riser/segregation consumers.
NO RCD upstream or at DB-FA1 per IEC 60364-5-56 §560.
ALL final-circuit cables FP200 LSZH for fire survival per IEC 60331 / IEC 60364-5-56 cable-survival category.

rationale.chat_summary: DB-FA1: 63A TPN+E fire-alarm sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, 6 final circuits (panel power + 3 zone loops + sounder/strobe + battery charger), fed from MSB-MAIN F04 with NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56 § 560.7.2. All Type C MCBs and FP200 LSZH fire-rated cables; panel design per IEC 62820. INFO flag: AHJ-required survival duration and secondary supply (ATS to genset) to be verified against the panel battery commissioning certificate at handover.

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: DB-FA1 is the dedicated fire-alarm sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, located adjacent to the building main entrance for fire-brigade and FA installer access. Constructed as an IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board (DBO) in an IP4X enclosure (indoor dry location). Role flagged as `fire_alarm_panel` so downstream SLD, riser, and cable-containment skills recognise it as a life-safety distribution requiring segregation, fire-rated cable, and no-RCD policy.

decisions.label: Specialty board role: fire_alarm_panel
decisions.summary: Flagged on the board block so downstream skills enforce life-safety routing rules.
decisions.rule: specialty_board role taxonomy — fire_alarm_panel marks the entire DB chain as life-safety per IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 scope.

decisions.label: IEC 61439-3 DBO assembly
decisions.summary: IEC 61439-3 Distribution Board class used — IP4X enclosure adequate for the ground-floor entrance lobby location.
decisions.rule: IEC 61439-3 — Distribution boards intended to be operated by ordinary persons (DBO) — assembly verification requirements.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 63A TPN+E intake at 400 V fed from MSB-MAIN F04 via a 60 m 16 mm² 5-core SWA XLPE Cu feeder. Declared PFC at DB-FA1 is 9.0 kA — attenuated from the MSB-MAIN bus PFC by the 60 m feeder impedance — well within the 10 kA Icn of standard MCBs. The 63A rating reflects the bounded life-safety load (≈3 kW total final-circuit demand + battery-charge boost transient) and intentionally provides margin for future addressable-loop expansion. Critically, there is NO upstream RCD at MSB-MAIN F04 per IEC 60364-5-56 § 560.7.2 — earth-fault trip would defeat fire alarm continuity, which is forbidden on safety services. Main switch is a 63A 4-pole switch-disconnector per IEC 60947-3 for emergency manual isolation only.

decisions.label: 63A TPN+E intake sized for bounded life-safety load
decisions.summary: 63A rating well above the ≈3 kW total demand provides margin for addressable loop expansion and inrush headroom.
decisions.rule: Life-safety supply must be sized for full panel boost-charge transient + future zone growth, not just steady-state load.

decisions.label: NO upstream RCD on the FA feeder
decisions.summary: MSB-MAIN F04 omits RCD protection because an earth-fault trip on the FA feeder would defeat fire-detection continuity during a fire event (cable insulation degradation can produce earth-fault leakage that does NOT compromise the alarm function itself).
decisions.rule: Safety services must remain energised during the foreseeable fire event — earth-fault auto-disconnection is incompatible with the availability requirement.

decisions.label: Declared PFC 9.0 kA at DB-FA1
decisions.summary: PFC attenuated from MSB-MAIN bus PFC by the 60 m 16 mm² feeder impedance — covered by 10 kA Icn MCBs without cascade backup.
decisions.rule: Fault-current attenuation along the feeder reduces required breaking capacity at downstream OCPDs; fault-level skill to confirm via formal IEC 60909-0 calculation.

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 6 final circuits: FA01 panel power supply (6A Type C, 1.5 mm²), FA02/FA03/FA04 zone detection loops for GF/L1/L2 (6A Type C, 1.5 mm², lengths step up by floor), FA05 sounder/strobe across all floors (10A Type C, 2.5 mm² for bank energisation inrush), FA06 battery-charger/standby supply interface (6A Type C, 1.5 mm²). ALL circuits use `voltage_class: fire_alarm` for downstream segregation enforcement (riser, cable-containment). Type C curves throughout to ride through panel SMPS + sounder capacitive inrush. All cables FP200 LSZH for fire survival per IEC 60331. NO RCD on any circuit — life-safety exemption.

decisions.label: All Type C MCB curves for FA panel + sounder inrush
decisions.summary: Type C curve (Ia ≈ 5-10 × In) accommodates capacitive inrush of FA panel SMPS and sounder bank energisation without nuisance tripping.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection per IEC 60898-1 — Type C suits transformer/SMPS/capacitive inrush profiles common in alarm electronics.

decisions.label: FP200 LSZH fire-rated cable on all FA circuits
decisions.summary: Fire-survival cable specified throughout — the FA wiring must continue to function during the foreseeable fire event for the duration mandated by the fire strategy (typically 30/60/120 min).
decisions.rule: Safety services cabling must provide fire-survival integrity for the duration required by the fire-safety design.

decisions.label: voltage_class: fire_alarm on every circuit
decisions.summary: Tags all DB-FA1 circuits as fire-alarm class so downstream riser/containment skills enforce segregation from LV power and ELV control cables.
decisions.rule: Voltage-class taxonomy drives segregation distances and routing rules in the riser and cable-containment skills.

decisions.label: NO RCD on any DB-FA1 circuit
decisions.summary: Earth-fault auto-disconnection is omitted across the entire DB-FA1 chain — RCD trip would defeat alarm continuity. ADS achieved by overcurrent protection only.
decisions.rule: Life-safety circuits must remain energised during the foreseeable fire — RCD protection is incompatible with the availability requirement.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Upstream MSB-MAIN F04 = 63A Type C MCB. Downstream MCBs at DB-FA1 are 6A and 10A Type C. Worst-case ratio at FA05 (10A) = 63:10 = 6.3:1; ratio at the 6A circuits = 10.5:1 with comfortable selectivity margin. Same-curve cascade (Type C upstream, Type C downstream) supports time-current selectivity per IEC 60898-1 cascade behaviour. Verdict pass — engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table at the 9 kA fault level. Fault-level skill to confirm full coordination study via the project-graph upstream link.

decisions.label: Worst-case selectivity ratio 6.3:1 at FA05
decisions.summary: 63A:10A ratio meets the threshold for Type C same-curve cascade selectivity per IEC 60898-1.
decisions.rule: Same-curve MCB cascade requires ≥1.6× current ratio + sufficient time separation for selective behaviour at the declared fault level.

decisions.label: Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table
decisions.summary: Selectivity declared by engineer at the 9 kA fault level pending formal coordination study from the fault-level skill. 6.3:1 same-curve Type C cascade ratio; FA panel + sounder loops fed from F04 incomer.
decisions.rule: Engineer declaration is acceptable per IEC 60364-5-53 § 536 when grounded in a manufacturer cascade table; formal IEC 60909-0 + tool calc to be appended at the runtime layer.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: DB-FA1 compliant against IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 (safety services), IEC 60364-4-41 § 411 (ADS by overcurrent — no RCD on the FA chain), IEC 61439-3 (DBO assembly), and IEC 62820 (FA detection panel + zone topology). The deliberate absence of RCD on the entire DB-FA1 chain is the critical engineering point: an earth-fault trip on the FA feeder would defeat alarm continuity during a fire event, explicitly forbidden under IEC 60364-5-56 § 560.7.1 (safety service independence) and § 560.7.2 (protection coordination). Protection achieved by overcurrent device only; residual shock risk acceptable in a controlled-access enclosure. One INFO flag: AHJ-required survival duration (typically 24 h standby + 30 min alarm) + secondary supply path to be verified at handover.

decisions.label: ADS by overcurrent protection only — NO RCD justified
decisions.summary: Life-safety exemption — earth-fault trip would defeat alarm continuity; ADS achieved by MCB overcurrent operation within the prescribed disconnection time at the fault level.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-5-56 § 560.7.2 permits omission of additional 30 mA RCD on safety services because the availability requirement (continued operation during the fire event) overrides the supplementary-protection rationale that applies to general circuits.

decisions.label: IEC 62820 fire detection panel governs panel design
decisions.summary: Panel internal architecture, zone-loop topology, and battery autonomy governed by IEC 62820 fire detection and alarm systems — this DB schedule covers only the upstream electrical supply.
decisions.rule: Scope boundary: db-layout skill covers the supply side of the FA panel; the panel internals are designed under IEC 62820 by the FA specialist.

decisions.label: Secondary supply path INFO flag
decisions.summary: IEC 60364-5-56 § 560 recommends a secondary supply path (ATS to genset) for life-safety distribution; current design relies on the panel-integral battery. AHJ to confirm whether battery autonomy meets the required survival duration.
decisions.rule: Safety services require a secondary supply path with autonomy matching the foreseeable fire-event duration; integral battery may suffice for smaller installations subject to AHJ acceptance.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 6-row table with 2 spare ways (W7-W8) reserved for typical FA-system growth: additional sounder/strobe circuit for atrium acoustic coverage, or interface circuit to access-control / lift-recall. NO RCD column populated across every row — the no-RCD policy flagged on every circuit so the rendered schedule shows it as a documented design choice rather than an oversight. FP200 LSZH cable specified in the cable column. Feeder route to be confirmed by the cable-containment skill — if the route passes through unprotected escape routes, the feeder cable itself must be FP200 LSZH (not just SWA XLPE).

decisions.label: 2 spare ways reserved for FA-system growth
decisions.summary: W7-W8 left as spare for atrium sounder expansion or access-control / lift-recall interface — typical growth pattern for commercial FA installations.
decisions.rule: Design margin per IEC 60364-1 Annex C — spare ways reserved at first installation reduces the cost of foreseeable future expansion.

invariants.evidence: INV-14 board DB-FA1 (INT → IEC): R1 jurisdiction→format mapping (INT/EU → IEC 60364-5-51 §514) holds; R2 board_label.text+svg populated, no {{}} remnants; R3 board_label.text matches IEC format regex; R4 6/6 circuits carry circuit_label.text+svg, no {{}} remnants; R5 every circuit_label.text matches IEC circuit regex; R6 tool_call_pending booleans set on all labels.
